title: Volcanic Eruption Dynamics

analysis.object: T239: Volcanic eruption dynamics depth analysis

pressure_buildup.reason: Exponential pressure accumulation from magma influx

supervolcano.reason: Chaotic, catastrophic, no finite EML model captures dynamics

seismic_precursors.reason: Harmonic tremor: Σ aₙ sin(nω₀t) = EML-3 oscillation

analysis.oscillatory_prediction: Can the framework distinguish which volcanoes are partially predictable? YES. EML-3 volcanoes (Stromboli, Old Faithful-type) have oscillatory eruption patterns — these ARE partially predictable using EML-3 models. EML-∞ volcanoes (Yellowstone, major calderas) have no finite pattern — unpredictable. The harmonic tremor (EML-3 precursor) IS the predictable signal within EML-∞ eruptions.

analysis.verdict: Eruption oscillation: EML-3. Supervolcano: EML-∞. EML classifies predictability.
analysis.theorem: T239: Volcanic Depth — EML-3 volcanoes predictable; EML-∞ supervolcanoes not

key_theorem: T239: Volcanic Depth (S518). Stromboli/Erta Ale: EML-3 (periodic eruption oscillation) → partially predictable. Yellowstone/Toba: EML-∞ (no finite model) → unpredictable. Harmonic tremor: EML-3 signal within EML-∞ eruption — the predictable island.
